rationale: The central home features a complex multi-hip roof with numerous intersecting planes. Ground shadows cast by the eaves extend approximately 10–15 feet (estimated via the 50 ft scale bar), indicating a moderate pitch. The ridgelines on each hip section sit noticeably inward from their respective eave footprints — the roof-to-eave ratio suggests meaningful elevation without being extremely steep. Roof plane surfaces are broadly visible from this near-nadir view, ruling out pitches above ~8:12, while the well-defined shadow articulation between planes and the inward ridge placement rule out pitches below ~5:12. Springfield, MO's Midwest climate and the architectural character of the home are consistent with a 6:12 estimate.
